profiles.title: Profiles
profiles.subtitle: The working-mode entry point. A profile switches model, provider, network, MCP, and safety policy together.
profiles.createHint: A diff is generated first. The config is backed up and written only after confirmation.
profiles.mcpSubsection: MCP tools referenced by this profile

providers.title: Providers
providers.subtitle: Manage model providers written into ~/.codex/config.toml. Subscription, official API, compatible API, and local gateway stay separated to avoid auth conflicts.
providers.createHint: A diff is generated first. The config is backed up and written only after confirmation. Enter an environment variable name, never a raw API key.
providers.networkSubsection: Network route attached to this provider

models.title: Model Config
models.subtitle: OpenCodex-style setup: enter model name, endpoint URL, and API key, then choose which models appear in the Codex picker.
models.visionHint: Enable Vision Bridge for text-only models: screenshots are described by a vision model before being sent to the selected model.
models.visionEnable: Enable vision fallback for this model

form.aliasHint: Supports provider:model, display=backend, and display->backend formats.
form.secretHint: You may paste a key here. Codex Box writes only an environment-variable reference, never the raw key, into config files.
form.restartCodex: Remind me to restart Codex Desktop after saving
form.restartAfterUpdate: Remind me to restart Codex Desktop after updating

models.byokHintBody: Most users only need two steps on this page: add a model config, then choose whether it appears in the Codex picker. Advanced provider routes, runtime injection, and diagnostics remain available from the sidebar.

providerRoutes.title: Provider Routes
providerRoutes.subtitle: Manage ~/.opencodex/providers.json. Each route feeds a group of model candidates into the Codex App picker.

codexRuntime.title: Codex Runtime
codexRuntime.subtitle: Read-only detection of Codex Desktop, Codex CLI, and ~/.opencodex/. Codex Box does not modify Codex Desktop internals and does not start any external gateway process.
codexRuntime.readonlyHintBody: This page only shows install paths, file locations, and last-read times. Codex Box does not patch Codex Desktop internals; the real BYOK path is provided by the Codex Box Runtime 127.0.0.1 local proxy. After Codex App re-reads ~/.codex/config.toml, it fetches the merged model list from the local proxy.

codexBoxRuntime.title: Codex Box Runtime
codexBoxRuntime.subtitle: Local 127.0.0.1 proxy that turns Codex App into a true BYOK picker. Routes /v1/models, /v1/chat/completions and /v1/responses by model id to each upstream provider. Inject rewrites ~/.codex/config.toml base_urls, Restore reverts them.
codexBoxRuntime.previewHint: Start the proxy first, then click Preview to fetch the merged model list.

conversationProvider.title: Conversation Provider
conversationProvider.hint: This edits the model_provider name Codex App uses to group conversation history. Actual requests still go to the 127.0.0.1 local proxy; backend_provider in the model catalog decides the real upstream.
conversationProvider.requiresOpenaiAuth: Keep requires_openai_auth=true for Codex Desktop OpenAI login compatibility.

codexBoxRuntime.injectHint: Rewrites every non-subscription [model_providers.*].base_url to http://127.0.0.1:{port}/v1 and records the originals in ~/.codex/codex-box/inject-map.json. Codex App will route ALL third-party traffic through this proxy.
codexBoxRuntime.restoreHint: Reads ~/.codex/codex-box/inject-map.json and writes the original base_urls back into ~/.codex/config.toml. Use this when stopping the proxy or rolling back.
codexBoxRuntime.routeTableEmpty: No providers injected yet. Click Inject base_urls to enable routing.
codexBoxRuntime.logsHint: Logs belong to runtime, not model config. This panel only shows redacted local runtime events.
codexBoxRuntime.sessionsHint: Sessions are separate from model config: models define available capability, sessions track runtime context.
codexBoxRuntime.safetyBody: Proxy only listens on 127.0.0.1 (never LAN). API keys are read from env vars at request time and never written to disk or logs. Every Inject/Restore goes through backup → diff → confirm → atomic write → rollback.

notice.injectOk: base_urls injected, restart Codex App to pick up new models

effectiveRouting.title: Effective routing chain
effectiveRouting.hint: Shows the path Codex App will actually use: model_provider → base_url/openai_base_url → model_catalog_json → backend_provider → upstream API.
effectiveRouting.empty: Effective routing has not been loaded yet.

diagnostics.title: Diagnostics
diagnostics.subtitle: Grouped checks for config, provider, BYOK dir, network, MCP, and backup with a redacted report.

settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: App-level settings for language, theme, safety policy, backup retention, log redaction, and BYOK behavior.

draft.title: Draft
draft.desc: All edits enter a draft first

backup.title: Backup
backup.desc: Save the original config before write

sectionDesc.byok: BYOK write master switch, schema preservation, and visibility sync

language.label: Language
language.desc: Follow the current interface language and switch anytime.

theme.label: Theme
theme.desc: Keep the light Mac Dashboard direction. Dark mode will be added later.

startupCheck.label: Startup check
startupCheck.desc: Read config and refresh the Dashboard summary on launch.

requireDiff.label: Require diff before write
requireDiff.desc: Every save action must go through Config Diff first.

maskSecrets.label: Mask secrets
maskSecrets.desc: API keys, tokens, and passwords never appear in UI or logs.

confirmDanger.label: Confirm dangerous actions
confirmDanger.desc: Delete, rollback, and overwrite actions require confirmation.

backupFirst.label: Backup before write
backupFirst.desc: Create a backup record before changing config.

retention.label: Backup retention
retention.desc: Keep up to 50 backups by default; settings will be editable later.

rollback.label: Protected rollback
rollback.desc: Rollback also backs up the current state first.

redaction.label: Log redaction
redaction.desc: Diagnostics keep route, status, and latency summaries only.

maxSize.label: Log size limit
maxSize.desc: Rotate logs when they reach the configured limit.

exportReport.label: Export diagnostic report
exportReport.desc: Reports are redacted by default and safe to copy.

byokWriteEnabled.label: BYOK write master switch
byokWriteEnabled.desc: When off, Models and Provider Routes pages are read-only and no writes hit ~/.opencodex/.

byokSchemaPreserve.label: BYOK schema preservation
byokSchemaPreserve.desc: Preserve unknown fields when reading/writing ~/.opencodex/*.json, so AITabby CLI stays compatible.

byokVisibilitySync.label: Visibility sync
byokVisibilitySync.desc: When toggling visibility on the Models page, sync the change into custom_model_catalog.json.

feedback.profileFormInvalid: Profile name, model, and provider are required.
feedback.profileExists: A Profile with this name already exists.
feedback.dangerBlocked: Dangerous action blocked. Real deletion must go through diff and backup.
feedback.providerFormInvalid: Fill in name, base_url, environment variable, and at least one model.
feedback.providerSecretRejected: Do not paste a raw secret here. Use an environment variable name such as OPENROUTER_API_KEY.
feedback.simpleModelFormInvalid: Enter model name, endpoint URL, and API key.
feedback.simpleModelSaved: {{name}} was saved and added to the Codex picker model list.
feedback.conversationProviderSaved: Conversation Provider switched to {{name}}. Restart Codex App to apply it.
feedback.opencodexParseErrors: Action needed: one or more model providers saved an API key directly in the config file. Codex Box is not using those keys, but it still shows data that can be read safely. Change api_key to ${ENV_VAR} and keep the real key in your system environment:
feedback.modelsEmpty: The model catalog is empty. A Provider is only an API source; add model entries before Codex App can show models in its picker.
feedback.openLogs: Log entry is ready; real log reading will be wired later.
feedback.mcpToggled: {{name}} enabled state updated in frontend state.
feedback.writeBlocked: Safe save blocked: real writes require backup + diff + atomic write.
feedback.rollbackConfirm: Rollback entered confirmation state and will not overwrite config directly.
feedback.modelDeleteConfirm: Delete model "{{name}}"? This removes it from custom_model_catalog.json.
feedback.subscriptionModelDeleteBlocked: Subscription default models cannot be deleted. Hide them by turning off "Shown in picker".
feedback.modelDeletePending: Delete requested for {{name}}. Click Confirm below that model.

dashboard.statusOk: Codex config looks healthy · Last backup 2 minutes ago · {{time}}

systemStatus.title: System Status
systemStatus.desc: Only key signals needed to decide whether the app is usable.

entry.title: Key Entry Points
entry.desc: Open the page for the task instead of handling details on Overview.

models.title: Configure Models
models.desc: Add API models and manage the picker

runtime.title: Runtime Control
runtime.desc: Start proxy, inject, restore, and inspect logs
